campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to accept a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local youth pay for recreation programs. The council also discussed plans to build a safer bicycle and pedestrian crossing over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
Plans to build a bike and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Council applying for grant funding to help pay for the Hamilton Avenue crossing project
Extension of an interim ordinance being considered

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipped the normal first reading step to speed up the process, with plans to vote on final adoption at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced to council
First reading waived to expedite the process
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to plan and organize upcoming council meetings. They reviewed what items should be added to the April 7 and April 14 meeting agendas, set specific times for discussing certain topics, and checked whether any agenda items needed special legal notices before being discussed publicly.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planned new items for the April 14, 2026 draft agenda
Set time limits for specific agenda discussions
Reviewed legal notice requirements for agenda items
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026, focused on approving previous meeting minutes and hearing public comments. The council also reviewed reports from various city committees and boards, and posted upcoming meeting schedules for community members to attend.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Upcoming board and commission meeting schedules
Recreation Division contact information for agenda questions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects in the city. The commission approved notes from their previous February meeting and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support local housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Federal housing grant applications being reviewed for funding
Community development projects seeking financial support
Human services programs and affordable housing initiatives
Approval of previous meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses thrive by discussing a program that lets businesses catch up on licenses without penalties, promoting shopping at local stores through summer events, and offering incentives to attract new retail shops and startups to Cupertino. The council also explored ways for the city to partner with private companies to sponsor community events.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - letting businesses get current on licenses without fines
Summer of Celebrations Shop Local Campaign - promoting local shopping with community events
Economic incentives to attract retail stores and startups to Cupertino
Public-private partnerships to fund and sponsor city events
Outreach efforts to shopping centers and local business owners

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a meeting on April 2, 2026 where the Historical and Landmarks Commission reviewed proposals related to historic buildings and sites in the city. Residents could attend in person or join online via Zoom to watch and comment on the decisions being made.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission deadline: 12 p.m. day of meeting
Historic preservation decisions for Santa Clara

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative tasks and approve several items. Key actions included approving previous meeting minutes, reviewing upcoming agenda items, approving travel for Mayor Montano to a conference, and confirming a school district representative for an economic committee.
keyTopics: Approved meeting minutes and reviewed calendar of future council meetings
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual conference
Appointed new Milpitas Unified School District representative to Economic Committee
City Manager given authority to negotiate and execute contracts (details incomplete)

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a meeting on March 23, 2026, where they recognized March as Women's History Month and approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities as disaster shelters. The council also approved a three-year contract to purchase email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster shelter agreement for city facilities
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report added to agenda
